title: Reframe as discrete-time remaining-stint modeling and convert to next-lap hazard
summary: This target is naturally a one-step hazard.
rationale: This target is naturally a one-step hazard. Survival stacking and discrete-time survival models often outperform plain binary framing when event timing matters, because they learn the full time-to-stop structure instead of only the next step.
implementation_hint: Create an auxiliary label for laps_until_next_pit within each driver-race stint history. Train either a survival-stacked classifier or a regression/classification model over remaining laps bins, then derive P(stop next lap) from the predicted hazard or survival curve and blend with the baseline classifier.
expected_effect: May improve ranking near pit windows by separating ‘not now but soon’ from ‘nowhere near a stop,’ which binary labels blur together.
risk: Label construction must be strictly causal and exclude post-stop leakage. Added complexity may not beat a strong direct classifier unless the hazard estimate is smooth.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
